tasks.title: Domain: pure token-bucket limiter with an injected clock
tasks.dod: src/rate-limit.js exports createRateLimiter({ max, windowMs, now = Date.now }) -> { take(key), size() }; take returns { allowed: true } or { allowed: false, retryAfterMs } with retryAfterMs strictly > 0; refill is fractional (elapsed * max / windowMs, clamped at max); a refusal consumes no token; buckets idle for >= windowMs are deleted by a lazy pass amortised to at most one per windowMs; no setInterval, no process.env, no import from express or from src/shorten.js

tasks.title: App: limiter middleware on `POST /api/shorten` → 429 + Retry-After
tasks.dod: createApp(db, { rateLimiter } = {}) accepts an injected limiter and defaults to one; the middleware is mounted on POST /api/shorten only and calls take(req.ip ?? 'unknown'); on refusal it answers 429 { error: 'rate limited' } with Retry-After = String(Math.max(1, Math.ceil(retryAfterMs / 1000))) and writes nothing; GET /:code, GET /api/links, GET /api/stats/:code and /healthz are untouched; the seed suites pass unmodified

tasks.title: App: env configuration and wiring
tasks.dod: src/app.js exports readRateLimitEnv(env = process.env) returning { max, windowMs }, parsing with Number and accepting only positive integers, falling back to 60 / 60000 otherwise; createApp's default limiter is built from it; src/server.js prints the effective pair once at boot because nothing in this repo loads .env; .env.example already declares both keys and is verified, not duplicated

tasks.title: Frontend: show the 429 and the wait
tasks.dod: on a 429 the existing #error paragraph names the limit and the seconds read from the Retry-After response header, falling back to a message without a number when the header is missing or unparseable; the URL field keeps what the visitor typed; no limit rule, no capacity and no window exists anywhere under src/public/; AC-07 verified by hand per test-plan.md

tasks.title: Tests: unit with fake timers + integration with an injected limiter
tasks.dod: npm run test:fast green; the fake clock is the injected now, never vi.useFakeTimers(); Retry-After is asserted against /^[1-9][0-9]*$/; a poll interval that does not divide windowMs / max pins the fractional refill; a 429 leaves the row count unchanged; every read path answers with an empty bucket; the sweep frees buckets without granting tokens; the three mutations in the checklist each turn a named test red
